column3: Objective 3.6 compares 110-120 VAC and 220-240 VAC input ranges.
column4:
Distinction

VAC describes the input side of the power supply, not the DC output rails inside the computer.

column2: The main DC output voltages listed for Objective 3.6 are {{c1::3.3 V, 5 V, and 12 V}}.
column3: Different components use different PSU output rails.
column4:
Distinction

This card tests the official objective's voltage set. Use Basic cards for deciding why the rails matter.

column2: A {{c1::20+4-pin}} connector can support either a 20-pin or 24-pin ATX motherboard power socket.
column3: The detachable four-pin section is attached when the motherboard expects 24 pins.
column4:
Distinction

This is the main motherboard power connector, not a PCIe auxiliary power connector.

column2: Watts can be calculated as {{c1::volts times amps}}.
column3: Wattage is the power rating used when sizing a PSU for a system.
column4:
Why

This supports wattage-rating questions without requiring memorization of a specific build's total wattage.
